preamble: The shift from the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] to the voiced labiodental fricative [v] is a form of labialization and fronting, often documented in historical developments where back fricatives are reinterpreted as labials due to acoustic similarities.

examples.note: Similar to the voiceless shift (enough), some voiced back fricatives shifted to labiodentals in specific dialectal environments.
Interaction between the uvular rhotic and labial segments in certain dialects, leading to labio-uvular or purely labial realizations.

sources: Portuguese dialectology research on rhotic variation.
